page: Amazon Web Services t3.large is a burstable performance instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 1 physical core, presenting 2 vCPUs via hyperthreading, and includes 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the t3.large in the lower performance tiers. Single-core stress-ng CPU performance is rated as weak at 1147.39, while multi-core performance drops to the poor category at 1669.29. Memory bandwidth tests show 93.13 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 12.87 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, both falling into the bottom 10% of tested servers. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET performance of 1.57 million operations per second reflect its entry-level, burstable design.

Given its burstable CPU allocation model, the t3.large is designed for qualitative cost efficiency in workloads with variable CPU utilization. It is suited for low-traffic web servers, small databases, development environments, and microservices that experience periodic spikes in activity rather than sustained, high-intensity compute demands.
description: Amazon Web Services t3.large is a burstable performance instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core, 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates entry-level performance, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics falling into the weak and poor categories, respectively. Memory bandwidth and database operations also register in the bottom 10% of tested configurations. This resource profile makes the t3.large qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads with variable CPU demands. It is best suited for light web serving, development environments, and small background processes that require burstable capacity rather than sustained high-performance compute.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3.large is a burstable x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, designed for cost-effective handling of temporary workload spikes.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3.large is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 1 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A burstable, entry-level virtual server designed for workloads with variable CPU utilization and moderate memory requirements.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB DDR4 RAM
Burstable CPU allocation model for handling temporary spikes in demand
Nitro hypervisor architecture with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Best suited for low-traffic web serving, development, and light database tasks
